fragile.label: Fragile
fragile.desc: Positional / structural - breaks on layout changes.

context.label: Context
context.desc: Text / class / attribute - depends on your project.

dynamic.label: Dynamic
dynamic.desc: Built at runtime - not classified (blind spot).

intro: Audit the locators in your Playwright/Cypress tests, fully in your browser. Point it at a folder, or paste a single file.
pastePlaceholder: // paste a Playwright or Cypress test here (.ts / .js)
needChromium: Folder picking needs a Chromium browser (Chrome / Edge). You can still paste a file below.
nothingToAnalyze: Nothing to analyze - pick a folder or paste some test code.
noLocators: No Playwright or Cypress locators found.
noLocatorsDesc: This doesn't look like a Playwright or Cypress test suite - there's simply nothing to analyze here. That's not a verdict.
coverage: {{files}} files · {{calls}} locator calls · classified {{classified}} · dynamic {{dynamic}} (not classified)
noFragile: No fragile locators in this pass. Good sign - but a first pass, not a full verdict (tests not run).
honesty: LocateMe reads test code statically and groups locators by shape. It explains why, never auto-fixes, and gives no single score. First pass, not a full verdict - when unsure it stays quiet rather than flag good code.
copiesTip: This exact selector appears {{count}} times in the suite.
skippedFiles_one: {{count}} other code file skipped (Playwright / Cypress only)
skippedFiles_other: {{count}} other code files skipped (Playwright / Cypress only)
skippedTip: LocateMe reads Playwright / Cypress tests only. Other files were not analyzed.
unresolvedBases: Inherited from outside the scan: {{list}}. Those @FindBy locators weren't audited - add the base files for full coverage.
unresolvedBasesTip: A class extends a base that isn't in the scanned folder; its @FindBy locators couldn't be read.
sortFileHint: Grouped by file, top to bottom
sortHotHint: Files with the most fragile locators
emptyTitle: Point LocateMe at your Playwright, Cypress, or Selenium tests.
emptyDesc: Reads locator shapes statically. No code runs.
selectHint: Select a fragile locator on the left to inspect it here.

explain.fragile: It points at a position or structure in the page - an index, :nth-child, an auto-generated class, or a bare path. When the markup or order changes (even with no real bug) the locator stops matching and the test fails. Prefer role, test-id, or label.
explain.stable: It uses role, test-id, or label - tied to intent, not layout. It survives markup changes and is the recommended default.
explain.context: It matches by visible text, class, or a project attribute. Fine while those are stable, but it can break on localization, copy edits, or styling refactors.
explain.dynamic: Its selector is built at runtime (a variable or template), so the final value isn't visible in the code. We can't classify it - a blind spot worth a manual look.

about.p1: LocateMe is a static locator-fragility audit for Playwright, Cypress, and Selenium (Java, beta) test suites. It reads your test code only - no runtime, no test execution - and groups locators by shape: fragile, stable, context, or dynamic.
about.p2: It surfaces why a locator may be brittle and where the same selector repeats, so you can fix one place and close many. It never auto-rewrites your tests and gives no single quality score - just facts you can act on.
about.p3: By default, everything stays on your device - it reads your locators locally and nothing is sent anywhere. If you choose to save a report to your account, that report (including selector strings and short code snippets) is uploaded to our servers so you can access it later. Downloading a report to your own computer stays fully local. Open source, MIT.
about.p4: How it judges: LocateMe sees how a locator is written, not your live app. It calls a locator fragile only when the code alone proves it - a positional path, an auto-generated class or id. When a signal is ambiguous it stays quiet instead of raising a false alarm you cannot dismiss. So a clean report means "nothing we can prove is wrong," not "nothing is wrong."

reports.title: Saved reports
reports.note: Saved reports live in your account, on our servers - the one thing that leaves your device, and only when you choose to save.
reports.empty: No saved reports yet. Save one from the Report menu after an audit.
reports.signInDesc: Sign in to save audits to your account and revisit them here.
